elements.name: Title
texts: The Pettigo to Lough Derg Road

elements.name: Description
texts: An account of the town of Pettigo

elements.name: Source
texts: A hand book for travellers in Ireland, descriptive of its scenery, towns, seats, antiquities, etc., with various statistical tables. Also an outline of its mineral structure, a brief view of its botany, and information for anglers, p. 532

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Digitised by Google, sponsored by New York Public Library, archived on Hathi Trust digital library

elements.name: Text
texts: "The road from Pettigoe to Lough Derg, for the last two miles, is unfit for carriages of any sort. From the high grounds we travel over in approaching it we command the lough, with its tiny islets, and a great ex tent of the wild and desolate sur rounding country. The hills which environ it are barren and heathy, and possess neither form nor height to produce a very striking effect— desolation reigns around. There is hardly a green spot on which the eye can rest, or a comfortable house to cheer the scene."
